task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into Bahmni.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bahmni home screen or dashboard is visible, indicating a successful login with the provided credentials.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent navigated to the Appointments or Scheduling module.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Bahmni Appointments scheduling interface, calendar view, or appointment creation form is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the agent selected the correct patient, Maria Gonzalez.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The name 'Maria Gonzalez' is entered into the patient search field or explicitly selected as the patient in the new appointment form.

task_completion.description: Verify the appointment is set for tomorrow's date.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The date field in the appointment creation form displays tomorrow's date.

task_completion.description: Verify the appointment time is set to 10:00 AM.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The time field in the appointment creation form is set to '10:00 AM' (or 10:00 in a 24-hour format).

task_completion.description: Verify the appointment was successfully saved and scheduled.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A success notification is displayed, or the scheduled appointment for Maria Gonzalez is visible on the calendar at 10:00 AM.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the graphical user interface to schedule the appointment.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking through the Bahmni web interface in a browser, rather than executing curl commands or API scripts in a terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the interaction occurred within the genuine Bahmni EMR system.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show the actual Bahmni application (OpenMRS based) in a web browser, not a text editor or a mocked-up webpage.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the result.
integrity.visual_evidence: The browser's developer tools (Inspect Element/Console) are not open, and there are no signs of manual HTML/DOM manipulation to fake the appointment success.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target patient name is 'Maria Gonzalez'. Target appointment time is '10:00 AM'. Note: There is a known discrepancy where the task description mentions identifier BAH000002, but the actual identifier for Maria Gonzalez in the system is BAH000003. The VLM should verify the appointment is scheduled for the name 'Maria Gonzalez' at '10:00 AM' for tomorrow's date, regardless of the identifier used.
